channel.title: Anime Architecture Archive
channel.description: Fictional Universe Intelligence System — power mechanics, factions, and system analysis for anime worlds.

item.title: Tokyo Revengers
item.description: A time-leaping delinquent rewrites bloody futures by navigating linear past events.

item.title: Vinland Saga
item.description: A deterministic economy of retributive violence

item.title: Steins;Gate
item.description: A deterministic attractor-field prison where convergence cannot be outrun — only rewritten

item.title: Sword Art Online
item.description: 10,000 players trapped in a death-game. Only victory frees them.

item.title: Tokyo Ghoul
item.description: A predator-prey economy where the ghoul/human biological boundary defines every alliance, motivation, and moral position

item.title: Re:Zero - Starting Life in Another World
item.description: Save-scum your way to victory — if you're willing to die enough times to find the winning timeline.

item.title: Solo Leveling
item.description: A fixed-rank hunter economy is broken by one player running an infinite growth protocol.

item.title: Spy x Family
item.description: A spy, an assassin, and a telepath form a fake family—each hiding their real job from the others.

item.title: One Piece
item.description: A world of maritime empires, ancient secrets, and the infinite chase for the ultimate treasure — where the network of alliances and debts is the actual power structure

item.title: One Punch Man
item.description: A hero who defeats everything with one punch comedically breaks the entire power-scaling economy.

item.title: Parasyte
item.description: A parasite takes over your right hand and becomes your most unlikely ally against alien invasion.

item.title: My Hero Academia
item.description: Mutating biological power collides with state-managed hero capitalism.

item.title: Mob Psycho 100
item.description: An affinity-matrix where psychic power distribution, spiritual resonance, and emotional pressure determine outcomes more than raw esper ability

item.title: Mushoku Tensei: Jobless Reincarnation
item.description: A reincarnation-driven fate system where mana growth, bloodline factors, and looping causality decide civilization-scale outcomes.

item.title: Naruto
item.description: A shinobi world where political loyalty, inter-village competition, and bloodline hierarchies define power more than any individual combat outcome

item.title: Goblin Slayer
item.description: A low-prestige extermination niche becomes the hidden maintenance layer that keeps a fantasy civilization from collapsing.

item.title: Hunter x Hunter
item.description: Contractual Power Economy & Asymmetric Information Warfare

item.title: Jujutsu Kaisen
item.description: Negative Energy Economy & Algorithmic Combat

item.title: Fire Force
item.description: Spontaneous combustion meets Breathing arts — Company 8 fights to save Tokyo.

item.title: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood
item.description: A closed thermodynamic system where every act of creation demands an equal destruction — and a 400-year parasite has been rigging the ledger from beneath the earth

item.title: Sousou no Frieren
item.description: A post-war fantasy where mana deception, lifespan asymmetry, and visualization limits decide who survives.

item.title: Dragon Ball Z
item.description: A ki hierarchy where exponential transformation tiers, power levels, and the brutal arithmetic of who can destroy what define every combat outcome

item.title: Death Note
item.description: An asymmetric information war where anonymous execution power collides with probabilistic deanonymization — and psychology leaks what cryptography cannot

item.title: Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba
item.description: A nocturnal immortality network versus humans burning their own lifespan for solar-grade kill windows.

item.title: Blue Lock
item.description: 300 forwards enter Blue Lock. Only one egoist survives.

item.title: Chainsaw Man
item.description: A fear-fed devil economy where control networks weaponize trauma, contracts, and identity erasure.

item.title: Code Geass: Hangyaku no Lelouch
item.description: An occupied nation becomes a control-war between imperial hierarchy, masked insurgency, and a will-hacking anomaly that turns strategy into governance engineering.

item.title: Bleach
item.description: A multi-faction spiritual network where Reiatsu dominance, Zanpakuto resonance, and dimensional boundaries between Soul Society, Hueco Mundo, and the Human World determine survival

item.title: Black Clover
item.description: Anti-magic versus elemental sovereignty — a kingdom bets on raw willpower over inherited bloodline.

item.title: Attack on Titan
item.description: A brutal deterministic closed-loop temporal matrix

item.title: The Determinism Engine in Attack on Titan
item.description: Why every freedom fight circles back to the same trap.

item.title: Why Devil Contracts Are a Raw Deal
item.description: Fear as fuel, trauma as currency.

item.title: Death Note as a Tool Critique
item.description: The notebook as a mirror for power fantasies.

item.title: Breathing Forms as Weaponized Poetry
item.description: How swordsmanship became a language system.

item.title: Why Nen Is the Best Magic System in Anime
item.description: Vow and Limitations as a self-limiting growth protocol.

item.title: Cursed Energy as an Inverse Heat Engine
item.description: Why Gojo's Infinity works like a thermodynamic paradox.

item.title: Chakra as a Bureaucratic Resource
item.description: How shinobi villages turned personal energy into a labor market.

item.title: Why the One Piece World Is Designed Like an Economy
item.description: The Yonko system, maritime trade routes, and Devil Fruit markets.

item.title: The Most Complex Anime Power Systems, Ranked by Structure
item.description: We ranked anime power systems not by flashiness, but by structural depth. From Nen to cursed energy to titan shifting — which system is the most architecturally complex?

item.title: Best Anime Worldbuilding: 5 Universes Where the World Is the Main Character
item.description: Some anime have worlds so structurally rich that the setting itself drives the story. These 5 universes have the most architecturally complete worldbuilding in anime.

item.title: LORE vs SYS Mode: How the Archive Sees Anime Differently
item.description: Every universe in the archive can be read two ways: as a story (LORE) or as an engineered system (SYS). Here's why that matters and how to use it.
